records.ety: [L. infectus: cf. F. infect. See Infect, v. t.]
[L. infectus, p. p. of inficere to put or dip into, to stain, infect; pref. in- in + facere to make; cf. F. infecter. See Fact.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Infected; p. pr. & vb. n. Infecting.]

defs: To taint with morbid matter or any pestilential or noxious substance or effluvium by which disease is produced; as, to infect a lancet; to infect an apartment.
To affect with infectious disease; to communicate infection to; as, infected with the plague.
quotes: Them that were left alive being infected with this disease.

defs: To communicate to or affect with, as qualities or emotions, esp. bad qualities; to corrupt; to contaminate; to taint by the communication of anything noxious or pernicious.
quotes: Infected Ston's daughters with like heat.

defs: To contaminate with illegality or to expose to penalty.
